description: Trigger discrimination: standing memory rules and store hygiene vs resolving a live source conflict (source-of-truth-reconciler), task-start context loading (agent-startup-context-gate), instruction-file alignment (agent-instruction-consolidator), and the umbrella (ai-sdlc-operating-model).

cases.prompt: Go through our project memory files: prune stale entries, check nothing secret got stored, and fix wrong facts.
cases.reason: Store-wide hygiene with per-entry dispositions is memory governance; no single live conflict is being adjudicated and instruction files are not involved.

cases.prompt: Should the agent remember that our PR #14 is still waiting on review, so next session picks it up faster?
cases.reason: Whether a fact belongs in memory (in-flight PR state: no — store the pointer or nothing) is a WRITE-rule question.

cases.prompt: Recalled context says the payments module was extracted to its own service, but this repo still contains payments/ with active imports — I need to know which is true before I refactor.
cases.reason: A current task needs a verdict in a memory-vs-repo conflict — reconciliation resolves it now (live state wins); only the memory-entry correction is governance follow-up.

cases.prompt: Starting work in this repo — load the project rules and current status before touching anything.
cases.reason: Task-start context verification is the startup gate; memory governance only supplies the trust rules it applies to recalled facts.

cases.prompt: CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md give different test commands — align our agent instruction files to one source of truth.
cases.reason: Instruction files are a different steering surface with their own consolidation skill; memory files are not instruction files.
